name: SendGrid
auth_notes: The primary method for authenticating to the Web API v3 is via API keys, which are managed through the Twilio SendGrid UI.
api_notes: The SendGrid API is extensive, covering mail sending, subuser management, contact/marketing management, and analytics/event webhooks. It primarily uses RESTful architecture (Web API v3).

evidence.claim: SendGrid is a cloud-based email delivery platform that allows businesses to send transactional and marketing emails programmatically via its API.
The SendGrid Web API v3 uses API keys for authentication and provides comprehensive endpoints for managing emails, contacts, and account settings.
Search citation: What are the requirements when using SendGrid as send method? : Customer Support Portal

access_notes: Developers can sign up for a free trial or free tier account themselves and generate API keys through the user dashboard. While the free tier limitations have changed over time, it remains a self-serve process.
one_liner: SendGrid is a cloud-based email delivery platform that enables businesses to programmatically send and manage transactional and marketing emails at scale.
mcp_notes: No official or widely recognized community-maintained Model Context Protocol (MCP) server was found.
